prompt_md: Your model provider is **deprecating the model** that powers your **fleet of ops agents** (on-call, CI, infra) — agents that take real actions in production. The same policy can make an agent more or less eager to act on the new version, and a silent behaviour shift means wrong actions in prod. You have an offline eval set of past incidents, real event traffic, and the ability to flag agent policy. **Pick the best rollout approach and the correct reason.**

decision_choices: Switch the whole fleet to the new model on the deadline day and watch for misfires in production

Shadow the new version in observe/dry-run against real events and the eval set, compare its proposed actions to what humans did, then canary it autonomously on a small slice behind a policy flag before full rollout
Refuse to migrate and keep calling the deprecated model until it stops working
Remove all the agents from ops to avoid the migration entirely
reason_choices: Because shadowing compares the new version's proposed actions on real inputs before any of them execute, a flagged canary limits the blast radius while you watch the misfire rate, and the policy flag gives a one-flip rollback if it acts wrongly
Because switching the whole fleet at once is the only way to find out if the new model works
Because a deprecated model will keep running forever if you ignore the notice
Because removing working automation is a reasonable response to a routine model upgrade
